titleStmt.title: Letter from Clerk of the Crown and Peace, County Dublin, to Robert Chalmers, 21 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Clerk of the Crown and Peace, County Dublin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
This letter was written by the Clerk of Crown and Peace, County Dublin to the Under-Secretary, Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938). In this letter, the County Clerk writes to Chalmers to confirm the appointment of the Justices of Peace on the Joint Visiting Committee for Mountjoy Prison.
Sir Robert Chalmers was appointed Under-Secretary of Ireland in 1916, replacing Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) in the aftermath of the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers 1916, NAI-CSO-RP-1916-10531

correspAction.persName: Clerk of the Crown and Peace, County Dublin

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Ref. No. 8983-15 21st June 1916. Sir, In to you Circular letter of the 24th. May last,
I beg to say that the Grand Jury, at the last County Commission
Court, appointed on the Joint Visiting Committee for Mountjoy
Prison, the Justice of the Peace whose names are set out on the
enclosed form. I am, Sir,
Your obedient servant Illegible signature
Clerk of the Crown and Peace. Thomas L. Plunkett D.L
Portmarnock House, Baldoyle
Thomas Clarke
28 Belgrave Sq. Monkstown
William Bobbett
Hansfield House, Clonsilla
Charles Stonwell, 22 Clyde Road, Dublin
